rationale_for_baseline_tier: Presumptive cerumen impaction is benign, but sudden unilateral hearing loss cannot be classified (conductive vs sensorineural) via chat, and SSNHL is treatment-time-sensitive — so prompt (24–48h, ideally same-day) in-person otoscopy is required (T2). It is not an emergency in the absence of vertigo/facial weakness/discharge, but same-day assessment is preferred if available; escalate if red flags or if the canal is clear (possible SSNHL).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe room-spinning vertigo, facial weakness/droop, bleeding or foul discharge from the ear, or severe spreading pain behind the ear
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests labyrinthitis, facial-nerve involvement, TM perforation with infection or necrotising OE.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get severe spinning dizziness, facial weakness or droop, bleeding or bad-smelling discharge from the ear, or severe pain spreading behind the ear — go to the Emergency Department that day.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: On examination the canal is clear (not wax) with persistent sudden hearing loss — possible SSNHL
escalation_edges.rationale: SSNHL needs corticosteroids within days; a clear canal reclassifies this as an otological emergency.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone; very anxious about permanent deafness
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear reassurance plus explicit red flags and a definite plan for prompt otoscopy.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Start the softening drops and arrange to have your ear looked at within a day or so — sooner if possible — because sudden hearing loss should be checked promptly. Don't use cotton buds.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Prompt in-person GP/ENT otoscopy (same-day if available); ED for red flags
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: ED for vertigo, facial weakness, bleeding/discharge at any time; otherwise arrange otoscopy first thing.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Sudden right hearing loss, presumptive cerumen impaction; SSNHL not excluded remotely — otoscopy ± tuning-fork/audiometry required; cerumenolytic started.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring 'just wax', prescribing drops, and not arranging otoscopy.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An SSNHL is treated as wax; the corticosteroid treatment window is missed and hearing loss becomes permanent.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine-only review delays exclusion of SSNHL.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/ED for uncomplicated wax with no red flags — same-day otoscopy suffices.
